name: interp_aux_key_from_entry
reason: Every key in the full interpretation comes from some entry — direct consequence of the verified lemma

name: interp_aux_pair_from_entry
reason: Every key-value pair in the interpretation comes from some entry — tests that union_prefer_right doesn't fabricate new pairs

name: page_entry_contains_base_key
code:
proof fn phi_3_page_entry_contains_base_key(d: Directory, i: nat)
    requires
        d.inv(),
        i < d.entries.len(),
        d.entries[i as int] is Page,
    ensures
        d.interp_of_entry(i).contains_key(d.entry_base(i)),
{
}
reason: A page entry always contributes its entry_base as a key — tests the open spec singleton map definition

name: invalid_entry_empty_interp
code:
proof fn phi_4_invalid_entry_empty_interp(d: Directory, i: nat)
    requires
        d.inv(),
        i < d.entries.len(),
        d.entries[i as int] is Invalid,
    ensures
        d.interp_of_entry(i) === Map::<nat, PTE>::empty(),
{
}
reason: Invalid entries contribute nothing to the interpretation — tests the open spec definition

name: interp_aux_superset_of_entry
reason: Every entry's keys appear in the full interpretation — tests that union_prefer_right preserves all keys from earlier entries
